The Evolution of the Entrepreneur with BumbleвАЩs Whitney Wolfe Herd

Podcast Episode # The Evolution of the Entrepreneur with BumbleвАЩs Whitney Wolfe Herd Confessions of a Female Founder with Meghan

Apr 8, 2025

44 min 33 sec

## Episode Description

Whitney Wolfe Herd is best known for creating the dating app, Bumble. She also happens to be MeghanвАЩs close friend. They chat about the pressures of launching a business and what itвАЩs like doing it in the public eye. Plus, Whitney explains her decision to return as CEO of Bumble вАУ and what she plans to change now that sheвАЩs bac

### Maggie Sellers On $50M Lawsuits, Fake Birkins, And The Future Of Beauty (Ft. Katie Sellers)

Video вАҐ Hot Smart Rich with Maggie Sellers Reum

In this episode of Hot Smart Rich, Maggie Sellers and Katie Sellers break down the five biggest stories shaping beauty, retail, and the startup lifestyleвАФ$50M lawsuits, fake Birkins, rebrand flops, and the future of K-beauty.We get into: The truth about K-beauty medical tourism (and what went wrong for one woman who lost her vision) Why Olive Young might eat SephoraвАЩs lunchвАФand what beauty from the inside out really looks like How Boy Smells lost the plot with their rebrand (and their core customer) How TikTok creators in China are exposing luxury markupsвАФand what that means for the future of fashion houses and consumer trust What the Revolve lawsuit reveals about influencer marketing, taxes, and the fine print no one talks aboutрЯУМ Like what you hear? Rate + review the pod to support women who are redefining what it means to build powerвАФthrough culture, capital, and consumer. ### The Samis Girl Chat: Friendship, Work, and Protecting Your Peace

Transform

The Samis are back for another Dear Samis episode, aka the ultimate girl chat, where they answer your burning questions. From how to stop feeling embarrassed about putting yourself out there online to how to ask for support from your best friends without feeling like a burden, nothing is off limits.Tune in as they open up about balancing work and friendship (and what theyвАЩve learned from navigating that dynamic in real life), protecting their inner peace in the middle of everyday noise, and the tools they use to tune out distractions and stay grounded. Plus, they share their death row meals and, to wrap it up, Sami spills on a few baby names she loves but wonвАЩt be using. ### How to Make Money... What We Learned from the Most Successful People in the World

The Burnouts with Phoebe & Sophia

Phoebe and Sophia detail the best lessons they have learned from the career playbooks of the most successful people in the world, from how to leverage relationships in your career to what makeup to wear. The Burnouts celebrate hitting 200,000 subscribers, and launching Phia, which has already reached one million users and raised a $35 million Series A. Thank you for watching, for subscribing, and for being part of this journey with us. Download the app and help us grow вЖТ https://www.phia.com About The BurnoutsThe Burnouts brings you raw, unfiltered conversations with todayвАЩs top founders, creators, athletes, and culture-shaping entrepreneurs. Hosted by Stanford roommates turned startup co-founders Phoebe Gates and Sophia Kianni, the show goes beyond the headlines to reveal the real stories behind ambitious careers. Past guests include Paris Hilton, Chelsea Handler, Kris Jenner, Karlie Kloss, and more. Subscribe for the insider playbook to build your dream career.

### What It Costs to Build a $12M-a-Year Grocery Store ft. Sammy Nussdorf of Meadow Lane

Financial Tea with Mrs. Dow Jones

The Brokeback Contessa aka Sammy Nussdorf, founder of New YorkвАЩs newest luxury grocery store, Meadow Lane, joins Haley to dish building in public, viral scandals, how much Meadow Lane makes a month, creating your own narrative, mythmaking in the press, and those viral chicken fingers. Mar 12

55 min 33 sec

* * *

### The RISE of Odd Muse: How Aimee Smale Redefined Modern Fashion

Rise with Roxie

What does it really take to build a global brand from nothing, without investors, without a safety net, and without losing yourself in the process?This week on RISE, Roxie sits down with Aimee Smale, founder of Odd Muse. What began as a university project and a dream on an iPad became a multi-million-pound fashion brand with stores in London and New York, sell-out collections, and a fiercely loyal community.But this conversation goes far beyond the highlight reel.Aimee takes us back to her very first week packing orders from a tiny self-storage unit, then fast-forwards to the pressure, responsibility, and reality of leading a 60-person team. She shares the risks that paid off (and the ones that really did not), how she built momentum after a single organic influencer moment changed everything, and the practical lessons she wishes someone had told her sooner, from pre-orders to operations to hiring support.Roxie and Aimee also get honest about the emotional cost of ambition. Burnout, overwhelm, anxiety, online criticism, and the strange loneliness of success. They explore how to protect your nervous system while still chasing big goals, why вАЬmoreвАЭ is not always the answer, and what it means to rise into the best version of yourself, not just a bigger business.New episodes every Monday.Follow us on socials: @risewithroxie
